MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Moton To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Darin Shaw, Esq. of SAC Attorneys LLP, 408.436.0789, appearing for Defendants Dilber Iraheta and Pure Remodeling, Inc.;
Atty. Andrew McClelland, Esq. of Lunski & Stimpert LLP, 415.426.3525, appearing for Plaintiff Stephanie Chan.

DEFENDANTS DILBER IRAHETA AND PURE REMODELING, INC.'s Moton To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' motion to set aside default and default judgment is denied in its entirety. Defendants' motion is untimely. "The court may . . . relieve a party or his or her legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her through his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect" so long as the application is made "in no case exceeding six months[] after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." (Code Civ. Proc., section 473.) The six-month limitation is jurisdictional and cannot be waived. "Accordingly, we hold that a court has no authority under section 473, subdivision (b), to excuse a party's noncompliance with the six-month time limit." (Arambula v. Union Carbide Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 333, 345.) The court entered default against defendants on July 28, 2020. (McClelland Decl. ISO Mt. Ex. E-F.) Defendants brought this motion on February 16, 2021, more than six months later. Defendants' reliance on Luxury Asset Lending, LLC v. Philadelphia Television Network, Inc. (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 894 is misplaced, as that case involved section 473.5, which authorizes a court to set aside a motion to set aside a default or default judgment if service has not resulted in actual notice to a party in time to defend the action and if the motion is brought within two years after entry of the default judgment. Here, Defendants' motion is not brought under section 473.5, they have not filed a motion to quash service, and they concede that they were aware of the action by November 2020.

Although defendants' motion is filed within six months of the default judgment, they are not entitled to relief. (Pulte Homes Corp. v. Williams Mechanical, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 267, 273 [where motion was filed less than six months after entry of default judgment, but more than six months after entry of default, because trial court could not set aside the default, "it also could not set aside the default judgment . . . , because that would be an 'idle act'"].) Defendants did not base their motion on equitable grounds. "As a general rule, the trial court may consider only the grounds stated in the notice of motion." (Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125.) In any event, Defendants make no showing of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Defendants' main argument is that they relied on their prior counsel to file a motion to set aside default. (Defs. MPA, 3:18-20.) Defendants then fired counsel, but relied on his representation that the motion had been filed. (Id.) When defendants hired new counsel, they learned the motion had, in fact, not been filed. (Defs. MPA, 3:10-23.) Defendants concede they were not diligent in making sure the motion had been filed. (Id. at 3:24.) Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiff?S Request For Default Judgment Against Dilber Iraheta And Pure Remodeling, Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Dilber Iraheta, an individual, and Pure Remoldeling, Inc., a California corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Ronald Lunski, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Stephanie Chan, an individual, who was placed under oath and testified.
Fai Wong, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Chan, and Messrs. Lunski and Wong, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants Dilber Iraheta, an individual, or Pure Remoldeling, Inc., a California corporation.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Negligence, Breach of Contract, Breach of Express and Implied Warranties, and Intentional Misrepresentation was filed on June 1, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendants Dilber Iraheta, an individual, and Pure Remoldeling, Inc., a California corporation, occurred on June 17, 2020 and was effectuated on June 27, 2020.
Default as to both defendants was entered on July 28, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on September 14, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Stephanie Chan, an individual, and against the defaulted defendants Dilber Iraheta, an individual, and Pure Remoldeling, Inc., a California corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $369,500.00.
Attorney Fees - Waived.*
Costs - Waived.*
Punitive Damages - $36,950.00.

Total - $406,450.00.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

* Plaintiff's counsel, Ronald Lunski, decided to waive attorney fees and costs rather than provide the Court with an itemization of said expenditures.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
